name: Fricassee De Poulet a La Poitevine (Chicken in Onion Sauc
recipeIngredient: 1 Chicken (1.5-2 kg [3-4 lb]), cut into 10 serving pieces: 2 drumsticks, 2 thighs, 2 wings, and 2 breasts halved
recipeInstructions:
The regional recipes of France have evolved over time to complement the local wines. Consequently, the rich recipes from Burgundy, Poitou, and Vendée are more homey but no less appetizing when paired with the region's lesser-known wines.
1. Peel the onions and slice them thinly.
2. Clean the chicken inside and out with a cloth wrung out in boiling water. Cut into serving pieces and season each piece on both sides.
3. Melt the butter in a sauteuse or large frying pan over low heat. When foaming, add the chicken and fry until golden brown on both sides. Remove with a slotted spoon and cover; keep hot.
4. Add the onions to the fat in the pan, season, increase the heat to medium, and mix well. Fry until golden brown, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking and burning.
5. Sprinkle half the flour over the onions and stir thoroughly before adding the remainder. Cook for a few moments, stirring constantly, then reduce the heat to low.
6. Add vinegar, stirring well to combine. Arrange the chicken on top of the sauce. Cover and cook slowly for about 40 minutes until the chicken is tender and the moisture from the onions has thickened the sauce.
7. Throughout cooking, shake the pan frequently and avoid lifting the lid except to stir the ingredients twice. Adjust seasoning if necessary before serving very hot with boiled potatoes.
Makes 6 servings.
